name: Hoz de Valdivielso — circular from Oña
name_original: Hoz de Valdivielso — circular desde Oña
region_intro: Touring cycling around Oña, located in the province of Burgos, Spain, offers diverse terrain within the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park. The region features mountain ranges, river gorges, and a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces. Cyclists can explore landscapes shaped by the Oca River and old railway lines, providing varied routes for different skill levels. Best touring cycling routes around Oña The most popular touring cycling route is Hoz de Valdivielso — circular from Oña, a 21.4 miles (34.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 43 minutes to complete. This difficult route navigates through varied topography within the natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Oña Historic Town Center – Trespaderne loop from Oña, a moderate 34.4 miles (55.4 km) path. This route offers a journey through the historic town center and surrounding areas, primarily on paved surfaces. Local touring cyclists also love the Panizares loop from Barrio San Pedro, a 23.2 miles (37.3 km) trail leading through the diverse terrain of the region, often completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Touring cycling around Oña is defined by the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park, the River Oca Gorge, and routes along old railway lines. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Oña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Oña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Oña?
A: There are over 60 touring cycling routes in the Oña area, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The region is particularly known for its diverse terrain within the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Oña?
A: Cycling routes around Oña feature a mix of paved and unpaved surfaces. You'll encounter varied topography, including mountain ranges, river gorges like the Oca River, and paths along old railway lines. Some routes are moderate, while others are more challenging, occasionally requiring you to dismount and push your bike.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Oña?
A: While many routes in Oña are rated moderate to difficult, the region does offer options suitable for various skill levels. For a moderate ride that explores the local area, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Town Center – Trespaderne loop from Oña&lt;/a&gt;. This route is mostly on paved surfaces and offers a journey through the historic town center.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to see along the cycling routes?
A: Oña is nestled within the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park, offering stunning natural beauty. You can cycle through the picturesque Oca River gorge or along sections of the old Santander-Mediterráneo railway line. For historical sights, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Center&lt;/a&gt; itself is a highlight, and nearby you can explore &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6318262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trespaderne&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Sal Salt Flats and Old Railway Station&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available from Oña?
A: Yes, Oña is a great starting point for circular touring cycling routes. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030219682/hoz-de-valdivielso-circular-from-ona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de Valdivielso — circular from Oña&lt;/a&gt;, which is 34.4 km long and navigates varied topography. Another popular circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Town Center – Trespaderne loop from Oña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Oña?
A: July is noted as a popular month for cycling in the Oña region, suggesting warmer, drier weather conditions are generally preferred. However, the diverse terrain means that spring and early autumn can also offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery for touring cyclists.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Oña?
A: The touring cycling routes around Oña are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the region, often praising the varied terrain and the natural beauty of the Montes Obarenes-San Zadornil Natural Park.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Oña offers numerous challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panizares loop from Barrio San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 37.3 km trail with significant elevation gain, where some segments might require pushing your bike. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31876418" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Sal – Red Rock Valley loop from Poza de la Sal&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 70 km.

Q: Can I find places to eat or stay in Oña after my ride?
A: Yes, Oña is a charming town with historical and cultural attractions, including the 11th-century Monastery of San Salvador. You can expect to find amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options within the town to relax and refuel after your touring cycling adventures.

Q: Are there any historical towns or villages accessible by bike from Oña?
A: Yes, several historical towns and villages are within cycling distance or can be incorporated into routes. Besides Oña itself, you can cycle towards &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6318262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trespaderne&lt;/a&gt; or explore the medieval charm of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frías&lt;/a&gt;, which is also home to a castle. The region's old railway lines also offer a unique historical context to your rides.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on touring cycling routes in Oña?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the route's difficulty. For example, a difficult route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030219682/hoz-de-valdivielso-circular-from-ona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de Valdivielso — circular from Oña&lt;/a&gt; involves over 650 meters of ascent. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31483597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trespaderne – Oña Historic Town Center loop from Trespaderne&lt;/a&gt;, can still have around 360 meters of elevation gain, so be prepared for undulating terrain.
tips: It is an interesting hermitage complex in the La Horadada gorge. Next to the road there is a stream and on both sides of it, a curious set of caves and galleries.

A tributary river of the Ebro River, it passes through the province of Burgos and is about 70 km long. It is born in the Montes de Oca, near Rábanos. The Oca River crosses Oña forming a narrow and beautiful gorge.
